mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls below 100k signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS declines below 100k despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and federal funds rate imply tighter monetary policy that raises equity discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise by >0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly expansion in real GDP above 2% signals strong economic growth that boosts corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle expansion regimes.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline by >1.5% in 60-day windows when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production above 105 points indicates strengthening manufacturing activity that drives equity earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative when INDPRO exceeds 105 points monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 1,000k signals weak demand for construction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST declines below 1,000k despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates a tight labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when UNRATE falls below 4.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative when both RSAFS and HOUST rise by >1% monthly.
